SWAT is a river basin scale model developed to quantify the impact of land management practices in large, complex watersheds. SWAT is a public domain model actively supported by the USDA Agricultural Research Service at the Grassland, Soil and Water Research Laboratory in Temple, Texas, USA.

div.div: compute all pairwise differences within a vector in R

Let's play golf abs(apply(combn(1:4,2), 2, diff)) @Ben, yours is a killer! > system.time(apply(combn(1:1000,2), 2, diff)) user system elapsed (11 more lines were trimmed) Who knew that elegant (read understandable/flexible) code can be so slow.
Creating a large virtual table in FROM clause

Here is a better way to create a table of values on the fly select * from ( (4 more lines were trimmed) Yet even better idea is perhaps to use foreign data wrapper to bring your mysql table in PG. EDIT You might want to try to pre-screen records from your main table provided it is indexed with ST_Dwithin(latlng, geom, 0.1) provided that 0.1° is just slightly more (mind cos(32.09)) than 10000 m.
